title: sagina subulata subsp. revelierei
intro: Sagina subulata subsp. revelierei, also known as Creeping Pearlwort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its small, white flowers and dense, mat-forming growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sagina subulata subsp. revelierei include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in a tincture or salve to apply topically.
